subject: Create readiness health check for readiness probe
commit_message:
Create readiness health check for readiness probe

New health_check.sh added that allows the readiness probe configured in statefulset.yaml to succeed

Change-Id: I3b619dfc84926bc27381c0b932971b41b12e4269
Issue-ID: OOM-1109
Signed-off-by: Aaron Hay <ah415j@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Sorry could you explain this change?  The old health check was just verifying that a required port was open.  The new one just echoes a string.  Shouldn't we be addressing why the health port isn't up?

Patch Set 1:

See JIRA OOM-1109 for more background information. This change is preventing the containers from going into a ready state until the startODL.sh script nears completion. It takes up to 15 minutes for APPC to be ready when an initialization occurs.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I can't accept this as a solution, as it completely bypasses the usefulness of having a readiness probe.  If it takes APP-C 15 minutes to start, then it's not "ready" to serve requests until that's done.  

When an APP-C pod fails, and a new one is started to replace it, the new one could immediately begin receiving requests that it isn't ready to serve.

Patch Set 1:

The existing port probe defeats the purpose of having a readiness probe, because it is indicating that APPC is ready when it really isn't. This change is actually making the readiness probe work as it was designed to so that the appc pod is not flagged as ready until it is actually ready to handle requests.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Was the issue that the readiness probe was marking APP-C ready too soon?  If so, that's likely happening because ODL is started twice in startODL.sh, and it passed readiness sometime during the initial up-time while features are being installed.  If that's the case, then you're right, it's not a very good readiness test and we should devise a better one.

With this solution, the health_check.sh file is created ( and available for the readiness check to run ) before the second time the karaf executable is invoked.  So there's definitely still an opportunity to advertise readiness before it's time.

Could we instead have a readiness test that hits an API which is only available after karaf is fully up the second time it's started?

I expect that SDN-C suffers from a similar problem, but it may not be as obvious since it installs fewer bundles and starts quicker.

In the end of the startup script it can touch some file. Readiness probe can check whether the file exists.

Patch Set 1:

@Borislav  That's basically what this change is trying to do, except that it's being done before the main ODL executable is invoked.  The karaf command does not return, it leaves you at a command prompt

Patch Set 1:

Prefer to merge this one at C release.
Topic set to casablanca

reviewing

Patch Set 4:

> reviewing

Please review patch set 4. I have verified that the behavior is working as expected in my testing.

The readiness status is failed until the completion of the APPC installation.

onap      dev-appc-0   1/2       Running   0         10m       10.44.0.7   k8s-appc1
onap      dev-appc-2   1/2       Running   0         11m       10.47.0.5   k8s-appc2
onap      dev-appc-1   1/2       Running   0         12m       10.36.0.9   k8s-appc3

After completion of startODL.sh and system level status is back to 100 after the ODL restart, the readiness probe succeeds:

onap      dev-appc-0   2/2       Running   0         10m       10.44.0.7   k8s-appc1
onap      dev-appc-2   2/2       Running   0         11m       10.47.0.5   k8s-appc2
onap      dev-appc-1   2/2       Running   0         12m       10.36.0.9   k8s-appc3

Containers:
  appc:
    Container ID:  docker://53ff2a51b51266d21bc478b561711f30dbc5a20170f8b4f555356955e449b935
    Image:         nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/appc-image:1.3.0-SNAPSHOT-latest
    Image ID:      docker-pullable://nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/appc-image@sha256:3f706dd64bc4edb91c54452af6f43cfa38e4813b20ac5c5572dffe35c7b1e186
    Ports:         8181/TCP, 1830/TCP
    Command:
      /opt/appc/bin/startODL.sh
    State:          Running
      Started:      Wed, 20 Jun 2018 10:27:31 -0400
    Ready:          True
    Restart Count:  0
    Readiness:      exec [/opt/appc/bin/health_check.sh] delay=10s timeout=1s period=10s #success=1 #failure=3




 > reviewing
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

This looks pretty good.  I'm just curious if it's possible for the readiness probe to pass prior to when ODL is restarted.  My guess is that it's possible, but that this window of opportunity is tiny.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

> This looks pretty good.  I'm just curious if it's possible for the
 > readiness probe to pass prior to when ODL is restarted.  My guess
 > is that it's possible, but that this window of opportunity is tiny.

It is not possible since the script is checking as to whether startODL.sh is still running. The startODL.sh startup script does not finish until the last ODL restart is executed; however, while the restart is occurring, the system level will not be 100 anymore and the health_check will continue to fail until ODL restarts and returns a 100 for the system level check.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

Is someone going to merge this soon?
